verses.text: 'The earth is the LORD's, and the fulness thereof,' for He founded it upon the seas. All things belong to their Maker. We are not owners but stewards in a world that is wholly His.
'The earth is the LORD's, and the fulness thereof,' for He founded it upon the seas. All things belong to their Maker. We are not owners but stewards in a world that is wholly His.

Who may ascend His holy hill? Only 'he that hath clean hands, and a pure heart,' free from idols and falsehood. Nearness to God requires holiness. Outward and inward purity alike must mark the one who would draw near.

Who may ascend His holy hill? Only 'he that hath clean hands, and a pure heart,' free from idols and falsehood. Nearness to God requires holiness. Outward and inward purity alike must mark the one who would draw near.

Such a one receives blessing and righteousness from the God of his salvation; this is the generation that seeks Him. Blessing follows the seeking heart. Those who long for God's face are themselves His gift and His delight.

Such a one receives blessing and righteousness from the God of his salvation; this is the generation that seeks Him. Blessing follows the seeking heart. Those who long for God's face are themselves His gift and His delight.

'Lift up your heads, O ye gates... and the King of glory shall come in,' the LORD strong and mighty, mighty in battle. The ascending King claims His throne. This pictures Christ, the victorious Lord, entering in triumph to reign.
